title: Uvaria oligocarpa
taxonomy: Uvaria oligocarpa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is a member of the genus Uvaria, which contains approximately 130 species of plants. The Annonaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the custard-apple family. This family consists of around 200 species of plants, many of which are found in tropical regions. The classification of Uvaria oligocarpa reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the Annonaceae family.

The leaves and roots of Uvaria oligocarpa are often used to make traditional remedies. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential applications of this plant.
